methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
BotBuilt, a Seed-stage company in Housing & Construction founded by Barrett Ames and Colin Devine, has raised $12.4M to date. The construction-tech sector contains two identified private peers (ICON, Apis Cor) and no public comparables, limiting the breadth of available benchmarking data.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation lever. Using the $12.4M total raised as a base proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $31M. This method assumes BotBuilt's capital deployment and traction support progression within a reasonable timeframe and multiplier range. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while Housing & Construction peers exist in aggregate, the narrow filter for same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables yielded insufficient data points (fewer than three), and an all-sector median would lack sufficient sectoral relevance to warrant inclusion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M (low) to $36M (high), with a median point of $31M. This range reflects the single-method constraint and the limited peer visibility in the Housing & Construction technology space. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x stage multiplier to BotBuilt's specific sector dynamics and burn profile, the lack of direct sectoral peer data for calibration, and the extent to which capital raised correlates with implied pre-money valuation at the Seed stage. Refinement would require additional Housing & Construction Seed and Series A comparables.
